good.text: Promises advance notice before terms change.
Says it does not sell your personal data.
You keep ownership and control of your content.

bad.text: The rules can change without telling you.
You give up your right to sue or join a class action.
They keep a broad license to content you create.
Your account can be closed at any time, for any reason.
You are tracked for third-party advertising.

clauses.label: Unilateral Changes
clauses.summary: Anthropic can change the published fee rates at any time, with the new rates becoming effective 30 days after posting or when the customer receives notice.
clauses.excerpt:
## H. Fees

H.1. **Payment of Fees.** Customer is responsible for fees incurred by its account, at the rates specified on the [Model Pricing Page](https://www.anthropic.com/pricing), unless otherwise agreed by the parties. Anthropic may require prepayment for the Services in the form of credits or offer other types of credits, all of which are subject to Anthropic’s [Supplemental Credits Terms](https://www.anthropic.com/legal/credit-terms). clauses.label: Forced Arbitration
clauses.summary: Disputes must first go through a 45‑day informal process, then be resolved by binding arbitration, with both parties waiving jury trials and class actions.
clauses.excerpt:
## J. Disputes

J.1. **Disputes.** In the event of a dispute, claim or controversy relating to these Terms (“**Dispute**”), the parties will first attempt in good faith to informally resolve the matter. The party raising the Dispute must notify the other party (“**Dispute Notice**”). The other party will respond to the Dispute Notice in a timely manner. If the parties have not resolved the dispute within 45 days of delivery of the Dispute Notice, either party may seek to resolve the dispute through arbitration as stated in Section J.2 (Arbitration).

J.2. **Arbitration.** Any Dispute will be d

clauses.label: Forced Arbitration
clauses.summary: Disputes are assigned to a sole arbitrator in Dublin for EEA/UK users or San Francisco for others, and the arbitration clause does not prevent seeking equitable relief.
clauses.excerpt:
## J. Disputes

J.2.a. For Customers residing in the EEA, Switzerland or UK, Disputes will be determined by a sole arbitrator in Dublin, Ireland pursuant the UNCITRAL Arbitration Rules as at present in force. The appointing authority shall be the President for the time being of the Law Society of Ireland.

J.2.b. For Customers residing anywhere else, Disputes will be determined by a sole arbitrator in San Francisco, CA pursuant to the Comprehensive Arbitration Rules and Procedures of Judicial Arbitration and Mediation Services, Inc.

J.3. **Equitable Relief.** This Section J (Disputes) does no

clauses.label: Unilateral Changes
clauses.summary: Anthropic can modify the terms at any time, with changes taking effect 30 days after posting (or immediately for legal changes), without needing the user's agreement.
clauses.excerpt:
## M. Miscellaneous

M.3. **Amendment and Modification.** Anthropic may update these Terms at any time, to be effective 30 days after the updates are posted by Anthropic or Customer otherwise receives Notice, except that updates made in response to changes to law or regulation take effect immediately upon posting or Notice. Changes will not apply retroactively. No other amendment to or modification of these Terms is effective unless it is in writing and signed by both parties. clauses.label: Broad Content License
clauses.summary: The clause lets Anthropic use any feedback you provide without obligation, effectively granting them a broad license to your submitted content.
clauses.excerpt:

A.1. **Overview.** Subject to these Terms, Anthropic gives Customer permission to use the Services, including to power products and services Customer makes available to its own customers and end users (“**Users**”).

A.2. **Third Party Features.** Customer may elect (in its sole discretion) to use features, services or other content made available by third parties to Customer through the Services (“**Third Party Features**”). Customer acknowledges and agrees that Third Party Features are not Services and, accordingly, Anthropic is not responsible for them.

clauses.label: Arbitrary Account Termination
clauses.summary: If the customer fails to pay, Anthropic can suspend or terminate access to the services and may pursue other collection actions.
clauses.excerpt:

H.2. **Taxes.** Fees do not include any taxes, duties, or assessments that may be owed by Customer for use of the Services ("**Taxes**"), unless otherwise specified in the applicable invoice. Customer is responsible for remitting any necessary withholding Taxes to the relevant authority on a timely basis and providing Anthropic with evidence of the same upon request. Where law provides for the reduction or elimination of withholding taxes, including via tax treaty, the parties will collaborate in good faith to do so. clauses.label: Arbitrary Account Termination
clauses.summary: Anthropic can suspend a customer's access to the services for various reasons, such as security risks or policy violations, and provides limited notice and liability.
clauses.excerpt:
## I. Termination and Suspension

I.3.a. Anthropic may suspend Customer’s access to any portion or all of the Services if: (a) Anthropic reasonably believes or determines that (i) there is a risk to or attack on any of the Services; (ii) Customer or any User is using the Services in violation of Sections D.1 (Compliance), D.2 (Policies and Service Terms) or D.4 (Use Restrictions); or (iii) Anthropic’s provision of the Services to Customer is prohibited by applicable law or would result in a material increase in the cost of providing the Services; or (b) any vendor suspends or terminates Anthro

clauses.label: Third-Party Tracking
clauses.summary: Anthropic states it uses cookies for necessary functions, analytics, and to collect data about users, including demographic and statistical information, which it shares with service providers.
clauses.excerpt:
## How Anthropic Uses Cookies

We use cookies to manage the Services and to collect information about you and your use of the Services. Cookies help us to recognize you, customize or personalize your experience, market additional products or services to you, and analyze the use of our Services to make it more useful to you. Cookies may also allow us to collect demographic and statistical data and provide this information to our service providers to help deliver their services.

We use cookies for the following purposes. You can also visit our [cookies table](https://support.anthropic.com/en/ar

clauses.label: Third-Party Tracking
clauses.summary: The clause explains that marketing cookies are used to target users, provide aggregated reports, and track when marketing content is shown.
clauses.excerpt:
## How Anthropic Uses Cookies

Marketing

Cookies help us market our Services more effectively to users that we, our service providers, and our business partners believe may be interested in using our Services. They also help provide us with aggregated reporting and help us know when marketing content has been shown to you.

clauses.label: Third-Party Tracking
clauses.summary: It tells users they can manage cookies in their browsers but notes disabling them may limit service use and that Anthropic does not honor Do Not Track signals.
clauses.excerpt:
## Managing Your Cookie Settings

Most browsers allow you to remove or manage cookie functions and adjust your privacy and security preferences. For information on how to do this, access the “help” menu on your browser, or access [http://www.aboutcookies.org/how-to-control-cookies](http://www.aboutcookies.org/how-to-control-cookies). Due to differences between websites and other technologies, you may need to take additional steps to opt out. Please check your settings for additional information. clauses.label: Notice Before Changes
clauses.summary: The company will inform you of any significant updates to its cookie policy and encourages you to check for changes when using the service.
clauses.excerpt:
## Changes to Our Cookie Policy

Anthropic may update this Cookie Policy from time to time. We will notify you of any material changes to this Cookie Policy, as appropriate, and update the Effective Date at the top of this page. We encourage you to review this page for updates when you access the Services.

clauses.label: No Data Sale
clauses.summary: The clause outlines various data‑privacy rights and explicitly states that Anthropic does not sell personal data, offering opt‑out options for targeted advertising.
clauses.excerpt:
## 4\. Rights and Choices

-   Right to know: the right to know what personal data Anthropic processes about you, including the categories of personal data, the categories of sources from which it is collected, the business or commercial purposes for collection, and the categories of third parties to whom we disclose it.
-   Access & data portability: the right to request a copy of the personal data Anthropic processes about you, subject to certain exceptions and conditions. In certain cases and subject to applicable law, you have the right to port your information.

clauses.label: You Keep Your Content
clauses.summary: The clause states that customers retain all rights to their inputs and own the outputs, and Anthropic assigns any rights in the outputs back to the customer and cannot train models on that content.
clauses.excerpt:
## B. Customer Content

As between the parties and to the extent permitted by applicable law, Anthropic agrees that Customer (a) retains all rights to its Inputs, and (b) owns its Outputs. Anthropic disclaims any rights it receives to the Customer Content under these Terms. Subject to Customer’s compliance with these Terms, Anthropic hereby assigns to Customer its right, title and interest (if any) in and to Outputs. Anthropic may not train models on Customer Content from Services. “**Inputs**” means submissions to the Services by Customer or its Users and “**Outputs**” means responses generat

clauses.label: You Keep Your Content
clauses.summary: It states that neither party receives any rights to the other’s content or intellectual property beyond what is explicitly granted.
clauses.excerpt:
## F. Intellectual Property

Except as expressly stated in these Terms, these Terms do not grant either party any rights to the other’s content or intellectual property, by implication or otherwise.

history.summary: Taxonomy update: 38 clause(s) re-evaluated under the current scoring rules. The document text did not change.
Taxonomy update: 34 clause(s) re-evaluated under the current scoring rules. The document text did not change.

The policy's clause on data sharing with third-party vendors was updated to remove the list of specific countries that data may be shared with. This change means that the company can now share user data with vendors in any country without specifying which ones. The change may make it harder for users to know where their data is being sent.
Taxonomy update: 6 clause(s) re-evaluated under the current scoring rules. The document text did not change.

Anthropic can now change its service fees at any time, with the new rates taking effect 30 days after posting or when the user is notified. The company also reserves the right to terminate or suspend user access to its services if fees are not paid on time, and may pursue collection actions. Additionally, users are no longer allowed to use the service to build competing products or train rival AI models. Users must also ensure their own users understand the limits of the outputs.

Anthropic updated its data sharing practices, now allowing the company to share personal data with affiliates, service providers, and other organizations for business purposes. This change may allow third parties to access user data. However, Anthropic also introduced more user-friendly elements, such as not selling personal data and giving users the option to opt out of targeted advertising. Users will also be notified of future policy changes and have access to summaries and prior versions.

The company updated its cookie policy to explicitly state that it uses cookies to collect user information for marketing and advertising purposes, including sharing data with third-party service providers. This means that user data may be used to target ads. The company also added a notice that it may update its cookie policy and will inform users of significant changes, allowing users to stay informed. No user-hostile changes were found beyond existing data collection and advertising practices.
